payload.text:
أحبّ أن أُنشئ تطبيقًا على الهاتف لتعلّم اللغة الفرنسية.
أريد أن تكون قاعدة هذا التطبيق مبنية على الإملاء (dictée)، وتكوين الجُمل الصحيحة والفعّالة، وتقوية رصيدي اللغوي بمعدّل 15 كلمة يوميًا، ومخصّص للمستوى B1–B2.

**Defining the French App**

I'm now focusing on the core functionalities for this French learning app. I'm prioritizing dictation, sentence building, and vocabulary acquisition. Dictation will need audio generation and text comparison. Sentence building requires effective construction, and 15 new words need to be added to vocabulary.


**Outlining App Features**

I'm expanding my app's features. I've decided to prioritize a global feel with an English UI and French learning content, maybe adding Arabic labels for clarity given the initial prompt. I plan to use the Gemini 2.5 Flash TTS for dictation audio and leverage it to pull 15 daily vocabulary words. Sentence building will involve word arrangement or translation exercises. I am now considering a home dashboard for progress.


**Refining Core Modes**

I'm now fully immersed in the core modes. I've broken down each mode: dictation with TTS, text input, and correction. Vocabulary mode involves flashcards. Sentence builder offers scrambled sentences or translation. I've also added an AI tutor for French practice. I'm leveraging React 18+, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, `@google/genai`, `lucide-react`, `framer-motion`, and `canvas-confetti`. I'm going to start with a metadata file, so I don't forget it later.


**Developing App Structure**

I'm now diving into the app's structure and layout. I've outlined the major components and files, including `App.tsx` for navigation and core state management and separate view files for the dictation, vocabulary, and sentence-building modes. I'll include the geminiService file to handle API calls. I also have type definitions, constants, a layout, and a root index. I've considered that the prompt focuses on writing, and decided to incorporate it within the tutor. I'll create a modern, professional style similar to Duolingo but using soft greens and blues. Finally, I'm considering multilingual support, including English and Arabic.

entries.description: Application configuration and permissions.
Main HTML entry point with Tailwind CSS and fonts.
React application entry point.
Shared TypeScript interfaces and enums.
Gemini API service for generating content, dictations, and TTS.
Main application layout with navigation.
The daily vocabulary builder component.
The AI-powered dictation (dictée) component.
The sentence building challenge component.
The main application logic and view routing.

payload.text: أرغب أن يكون هذا التطبيق مرتبطًا دائمًا بالاتصال بالإنترنت وأن يكون محتواه قابلًا للتحديث باستمرار.

**Examining Constant Connectivity**

I'm currently focused on the requirements for the French learning app: ensuring a persistent internet connection and constantly updatable content. I'm taking a closer look at `VocabularyView`, to ensure fetching vocab happens in the background, and am planning a full refresh implementation to pull in new content.


**Analyzing Real-Time AI**

I've been thinking about the application's dynamism and how to satisfy the "always connected" and "constantly updatable" user requests. I'm prioritizing the real-time AI Tutor feature using the Gemini Live API. I'm also planning to incorporate a connection status indicator and emphasize content refresh options within the app's UI. The aim is to create a seamless, real-time French learning experience.


**Developing the Tutor Interface**

I'm now fully focused on creating a live, interactive Tutor interface using the Gemini API's chat functionality. I'm updating `App.tsx` and the other associated parts, like `services/gemini.ts` and `Dashboard`, to handle the actual chat logic and visual "live" feedback, like a pulse indicator and refined chat interface. I need to ensure a detailed feedback loop using the latest AI capabilities, and improve content loading with better skeletons to provide reassurance to the user.

entries.description: Added ChatMessage interface for the AI Tutor view.
Added chat functionality and updated prompts for a more dynamic "always connected" feel.
Updated Layout with a "Live Sync" indicator to emphasize cloud connectivity.
New interactive AI Tutor component leveraging Gemini Chat for a live experience.
Integrated TutorView and added a "Cloud Updated" notice to Dashboard.
Added lucide-react icons used in new components to the import map.

payload.text: Implement functionality in the VocabularyView to allow users to save words they want to review later. Add a 'Saved Words' section accessible from the dashboard.

**Considering Feature Scope**

Okay, I'm thinking about the "Saved Words" feature for the French learning app. Right now, I'm focusing on implementation details. First, I will update `types.ts`. I need to figure out how to best represent the saved words in the data structure and how to persist this data. I will add a new view to the dashboard. I plan on making the new section accessible from the dashboard.


**Defining Data Structures and Views**

I've been defining the structure and views needed for the "Saved Words" feature. I've updated the `types.ts` file to include the `SAVED_WORDS` view. Now, I'm focusing on the implementation details for `VocabularyView.tsx`, including adding save/unsave logic and using `localStorage` for persistent storage of saved words as per the request. I plan to create the `SavedWordsView.tsx` component next. I'll make sure it is linked from the dashboard, as specified.

entries.description: Add SAVED_WORDS to AppView enum.
Implement word saving functionality with persistence in localStorage.
Create a new view for reviewing saved vocabulary words.
Add SavedWordsView to the app router and include a link from the dashboard.
